editor.description: Choose an execution engine and link an LLM provider. CLI backends route requests through the local HTTP proxy, so claude/codex login is not required.

permission.bypassWarning: New sessions start by bypassing the permission gate. Runtime can still switch among the 4 modes. Use only in isolated sandboxes or CI.
permission.label: Default Permission Mode
permission.remoteRootHintPrefix: When remote agentred runs as root/sudo, claude CLI rejects bypassPermissions as --dangerously-skip-permissions. Set
permission.remoteRootHintSuffix: to let the CLI skip that check.

provider.label: LLM Provider
provider.noMatchClaude: Without a link, claude CLI uses its own login. Add an anthropic provider first if requests should route through the local proxy.
provider.noMatchCodex: Without a link, codex CLI uses its own login. Add an openai-response provider first if requests should route through the local proxy.
provider.noneDescription: Add an LLM provider on the LLM Providers page first, then return to configure the backend.
provider.piAgentDescription: Pi Agent reads local Pi configuration and authentication. It is not linked to an Agentre LLM Provider.
provider.staleOptionalClause: , or clear the link to use CLI login
provider.staleTitle: The original LLM provider is disabled or does not match this type

providerSync.descriptionOnly: Sync writes the locally saved API key to the remote agentred state file. Other form changes are not saved.
providerSync.descriptionSave: The selected remote agentred does not have these providers configured. Sync writes the locally saved API key to the remote agentred state file, then continues saving the agent backend.
providerSync.inlineDescription: This backend will run on remote agentred. You can sync the selected LLM provider to the remote state file first.
providerSync.requiredDescription: The remote runtime can only read providers from its own state file. Unsynced providers trigger provider not configured.
providerSync.secretServiceAction: Fix: upgrade and restart remote agentred. The current version writes directly to the agentred state file and no longer uses the keychain. If you cannot upgrade yet, install and start a Secret Service such as gnome-keyring or KWallet on the remote machine.
providerSync.secretServiceMissing: An older remote agentred is still writing to the system keychain, but remote Linux has no available Secret Service (org.freedesktop.secrets).
providerSync.title: Sync Remote LLM Provider

proxy.notRunning: Local HTTP proxy is not running
proxy.running: Requests route through the local HTTP proxy ({{url}}). Secrets stay inside the app.
proxy.unlinked: No provider is linked. The CLI uses its own login state, and secrets do not pass through the app.

disabled.bypassAfterLaunch: This session did not start with bypassPermissions. Claude CLI does not allow entering bypass mid-session. Start a new session with bypass selected to unlock two-way switching.

permissionMode.footer: Cannot switch while generating · selection before send applies to the next turn
permissionMode.heading: Permission Mode

acceptEdits.desc: Automatically accept file edits; other tools still ask

bypassPermissions.desc: Skip all permission gates; when selected before the first send, the CLI starts with --permission-mode bypassPermissions

default.desc: Ask before each tool call and trigger can_use_tool

plan.desc: Read-only analysis without write or side-effect tools

permissionMode.titleDisabled:
Permission mode · {{label}}

compact.description: Compact the chat context and keep only the summary

goal.description: Set or view the current Codex goal

agentBackend.description: Configure agent execution engines and their models. Runtime options such as working directory and permission mode are configured per agent in Organization → Agent.

runtimeHint.description: Working directory, permission mode, extra CLI arguments, and related runtime options are configured per agent under Organization → Agent → Config. This page only manages shared backend instances.
runtimeHint.title: Runtime options live on each agent

agentBackend.title: Agent Backends

colorMode.description: Follow the system appearance or pin the interface to light or dark mode.
colorMode.title: Color Mode

appearance.description: Adjust how Agentre is displayed. Theme and language preferences are saved on this device.
appearance.title: Appearance

localProxy.description: Configure the app's built-in LLM Gateway. claudecode and codex subprocesses send HTTP requests here; the app authenticates them with a temporary token and forwards requests to the real LLM provider without exposing secrets outside the app.
localProxy.title: Local HTTP Proxy

llmProvider.description: Configure available model sources. After saving, Agentre can fetch the account's available models, while context window, thinking support, and other capabilities are filled from the built-in cago agents catalog.
llmProvider.title: LLM Providers

mcpServers.description: The MCP server configuration view is reserved for managing connections, authorization, and available tools.
mcpServers.title: MCP Servers

notifications.description: The notification settings view is reserved for reminder rules and delivery channels.
notifications.title: Notifications

skillsTools.description: The skills and tools management view is reserved for enabling, disabling, and configuring agent capabilities.
skillsTools.title: Skills / Tools

flash.savedButStopped: Settings saved, but the proxy did not start: {{reason}}

listener.description: All LLM requests from claudecode / codex subprocesses arrive here. The app routes them to real LLM providers with tokens.
listener.portHint: Default 52401. Use 0 for a random port on each launch.
listener.title: Listener

routes.description: Requests are forwarded after temporary token verification. Tokens live only in memory during tests or runtime.
routes.mcpReserved: Reserved local MCP service entry point (404 when no service is registered)
routes.title: Exposed Routes

security.description: Real LLM provider secrets stay in main-process memory. CLI subprocesses receive temporary tokens issued by the app. Tokens are valid for only 60 seconds, revoked immediately after tests, and never written to .env, config, or process args.
security.title: Secrets Stay in the App

conflict.systemReserved: This chord is reserved by the system. Choose another one.
